text.title: Spatial Accessibility Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns repeatedly observed in spatial-product audits. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Interactions assume Quest Touch controllers; hand-tracking-only Vision Pro users cannot complete grab/select; store review rejects submission.

root-cause.source: Internal — observed in cross-platform XR ports

antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to controller-only assumptions without enumerating alternatives; LLMs trained on older XR docs amplify the bias.
antipattern.fix: Run `templates/alt-input-matrix.ts` audit. Every interaction must list ≥2 modalities. Add explicit hand-tracking + voice paths where controllers are primary.

antipattern.symptom: Always-visible HUD locked to head. Vision Pro store review fails ("UI must not be head-locked"); Quest users report nausea.

root-cause.source: Apple HIG visionOS — head-locked content forbidden

antipattern.root-cause: Author followed legacy XR docs from pre-visionOS era; LLM recommended head-locked from training data.
antipattern.fix: Use head-locked-with-decay or world-locked + recenter affordance. Head-locked allowed only for critical telemetry (battery, safety warnings).

antipattern.symptom: Compliance declared from automated checklists; first real user with vestibular disorder experiences nausea on day-1 demo.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated synthetic XR a11y tools as sufficient; comfort thresholds tuned to designer baseline, not user variance.
antipattern.fix: Recruit ≥1 low-vision + 1 motor-impaired + 1 motion-sensitive tester per major release. Block compliance claim until hardware testing log exists.

antipattern.symptom: Captions default to world-locked at 5 m; deaf user in moving scene cannot read; complaints filed.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to "immersive" placement without considering motion legibility.
antipattern.fix: Default captions to head-locked ~3 m. Expose world-locked as an opt-in with size + contrast slider. Audio descriptions mandatory for visually-encoded meaning.
